body.title: Exit habit misfires on imperative phrases like "run it yourself"
body.result: Two root causes fixed: (1) author_filter stored as list ['akien','user'] was broken — string vs list comparison always True, habit disabled for all authors; fixed to support both string and list formats in basal_ganglia.select_habit. (2) PROC_EXIT_IGOR used space-separated trigger; 'yourself' (len 8) matched 'run it yourself'; converted to pipe-separated phrases in live DB, added 'web-user' to author_filter. 13 tests in test_exit_habit_misfire.py. Commit 153f96e.
body.description: Igor wrote exit flag in response to CC message containing "Run it yourself." Trigger for exit/restart habit is too broad — matching short imperative phrases it should not. Audit exit/restart habit triggers and tighten them. Also audit author_filter — exit habit should not fire on claude-code author messages at all.
